message:
fix(util/resource): deallocate debug_asserts, reset_stats preservation, within_limits cleanup

Three targeted fixes to the resource tracking subsystem:

1. SymbolPool::deallocate() gains two debug_assert!s:
   - Asserts the symbol is currently marked in_use before deallocation
   - Asserts the symbol_size matches the pool's configured symbol_size
   These catch double-free and cross-pool deallocation bugs in debug builds
   without runtime overhead in release builds.

2. reset_stats() now preserves current_usage and peak_usage instead of
   zeroing them. These fields reflect actual allocated resources, not
   accumulated counters — resetting them to zero would misrepresent the
   pool's actual state. Only the operational counters should be reset.

3. within_limits() removes the max_per_object_memory check. Per-object
   memory limits are a call-site concern (checked when allocating individual
   objects), not a global resource-level constraint. Checking it here
   produced false positives when total memory was within limits but a
   single object's size exceeded the per-object cap.

Tests: reset_stats_preserves_current_and_peak_usage and
within_limits_ignores_per_object_memory verify the new behaviors.

test(supervision): add tolerance field to StormMonitorConfig in tests

Adds the required `tolerance: 1.2` field to all existing StormMonitorConfig
struct literals in test functions. This is a follow-up to the tolerance
field addition (feat(supervision): add configurable tolerance) to ensure
all test configs compile with the new required field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
